_description: Named reaction lookup: maps common reaction names to their implied reagents, solvents, and NMR-relevant compounds that may appear in crude spectra. The agent should use this to determine what additional reference spectra to fetch beyond the stated reactants and ReactionT5-predicted products.
_usage: When a user describes a reaction by name (e.g., 'hydroboration of X'), look up the reaction name here. Fetch NMR references for: (1) stated reactants, (2) ReactionT5-predicted products, (3) any solvents/reagents from this table that are NMR-visible and likely present in the crude mixture.

hydroboration.notes: Usually followed by oxidation (H2O2/NaOH) to give alcohol. Without oxidation, intermediate is trialkylborane. THF is the most common solvent and will dominate the NMR if not removed.

hydroboration-oxidation.notes: Anti-Markovnikov addition of OH. Products are alcohols.

grignard.notes: Grignard reagent + carbonyl -> alcohol after aqueous workup. Ether solvents are very NMR-visible.

wittig.notes: Produces alkene + Ph3P=O (triphenylphosphine oxide). Ph3P=O is a common NMR-visible byproduct.

aldol.notes: Can be acid or base catalyzed. Solvent depends on conditions.

diels-alder.notes: Thermal [4+2] cycloaddition. Often run neat or in non-polar solvent.

fischer-esterification.notes: Acid-catalyzed: carboxylic acid + alcohol -> ester + water.

suzuki-coupling.notes: Pd-catalyzed cross-coupling: ArB(OH)2 + ArX -> Ar-Ar. DMF and dioxane are common NMR contaminants.

heck-reaction.notes: Pd-catalyzed: ArX + alkene -> substituted alkene. Triethylamine base is NMR-visible.

sonogashira-coupling.notes: Pd/Cu-catalyzed: ArX + terminal alkyne -> internal alkyne.

nabh4-reduction.notes: Mild reducing agent for ketones/aldehydes -> alcohols. Protic solvents (MeOH, EtOH) are very NMR-visible.

lialh4-reduction.notes: Strong reducing agent. Strictly anhydrous conditions, ether solvents.

jones-oxidation.notes: Oxidizes primary/secondary alcohols. Acetone is both solvent and NMR-visible.

swern-oxidation.notes: Mild oxidation of alcohols to aldehydes/ketones. DMSO is a key reagent AND NMR contaminant.

dess-martin-oxidation.notes: Mild oxidation. Acetic acid is a byproduct and NMR-visible.

epoxidation.notes: mCPBA epoxidation produces m-chlorobenzoic acid byproduct (NMR-visible).

bromination.notes: Br2 in CCl4 for alkene bromination; NBS for allylic/benzylic. CCl4 is NMR-silent.

friedel-crafts-acylation.notes: Lewis acid catalyzed. Nitrobenzene solvent is NMR-visible.

mitsunobu.notes: Inverts alcohol stereochemistry. PPh3=O and reduced DIAD/DEAD are NMR-visible byproducts.

olefin-metathesis.notes: RCM/CM/ROMP. Ethylene is a gaseous byproduct (usually removed).

amide-coupling.notes: Peptide/amide bond formation. DMF and DIPEA are common NMR contaminants.

birch-reduction.notes: Dissolving metal reduction of aromatics. t-BuOH is a proton source and NMR-visible.

williamson-ether-synthesis.notes: Alkoxide + alkyl halide -> ether. SN2 mechanism.

_common_nmr_solvents._description: Common NMR-visible solvents and their 1H chemical shifts. If any of these appear as reaction solvents, their signals will likely be present in crude mixture NMR unless the sample was dried/stripped.
